Receipt OCR Pipeline

Claude Vision extracts structured data from 100 multilingual cafe supplier receipts. 17 representative results shown below covering all edge cases.

100
Receipts Processed
318
Line Items Extracted
96.7%
Avg OCR Confidence
94.9%
Ingredient Mapping
12
Menu Items Costed

8-Stage Async Pipeline

Each receipt flows through these stages with structured Pydantic contracts between them

1 OCR
2 Validate
3 Normalize
4 Classify
5 Map
6 Calculate
7 Persist
8 Report

Edge Cases Handled

Real-world supplier invoices come with surprises. Here's what the pipeline handles.

🇩🇪 🇫🇷 🇪🇸
Multi-Language OCR
German, French, Spanish invoices with localized product names
Pipeline: LLM maps "Espresso Mischung" to coffee_beans, "Leche Entera" to whole_milk
🔄
Credit Notes & Refunds
Negative totals from returned goods - common in supplier relationships
Pipeline: Detects negative totals, marks as credit note, adjusts cost averaging
💰
Discounts & Fees
Percentage discounts, delivery charges, bulk pricing
Pipeline: is_discount and is_tax_or_fee flags exclude from ingredient costs
💱
Multi-Currency
USD, GBP invoices alongside EUR - fixed-rate conversion
Pipeline: Normalizes to EUR at configurable rates (USD=0.92, GBP=1.17)
🧹
Non-COGS Filtering
Cleaning supplies, napkins, straws mixed with food ingredients
Pipeline: Classification stage separates COGS from operational supplies
📦
Bulk & Mixed Orders
5+ items per invoice, mixed categories, varying units
Pipeline: Unit normalization handles kg, L, box/12, 6pk, 500ml, etc.

Receipt Gallery

Click any image to zoom. Toggle raw JSON to see the exact Claude Vision output.
